title: About the Building Activity Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the Building Activity Survey
21 September 2023, 10:45am
Information about taking part in the Building Activity Survey, such as why we carry out the survey, how we selected you, and how to complete it.
We carry out this survey to provide a quarterly measure on the value of work done on building jobs in New Zealand.
We select all residential and non-residential building jobs in the highest building-consent value group, along with all staged consents. Building consents issued by city and district councils (territorial authorities), excluding consents for non-building construction and temporary constructions, may be included in the survey.
You may be selected if your building activity fits the criteria above, and if you are the applicant (eg homeowner, building contractor, or project manager) named on a building consent issued by a city or district council (territorial authorities).
The applicant receives a survey each quarter until the building job is reported as finished.
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the Building Activity Survey.
The survey is carried out every quarter.

## How to complete the survey
You can complete your survey online. This is a quick, easy, and secure way to complete the survey. Find more information by visiting Completing your Quarterly Building Activity Survey .
Most computers and browsers can be used to complete the survey online. Supported operating and browsers for desktop access include:
- Windows 10, Windows 11 Edge Firefox Chrome
- Mac OS Firefox Chrome Safari
Supported browsers for online mobile device:
- Android Chrome UC Browser
The survey has been designed to be done on a desktop computer but tablets and smartphones can also be used.
- Business survey information 31 July 2017
- Individual and household survey information 01 January 2017
- Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data 20 May 2026
- Building 01 January 2017 Image: construction house
- Contact us for survey help 26 May 2020
